__fixture__.name: start_center_restore_card_compatible
__fixture__.scenario: Reopen after a minor extension-version bump and one language-server host restart. The restore card summarises the upstream restore_prompt_record (see fixtures/workspace/entry_restore_examples/restore_last_session.json) without flattening recovery classes into a single Reopen CTA. restore_level = compatible_restore is quoted verbatim. summary_counts separates exact_restore_item_count (3), dirty_buffer_recovery_item_count (2 dirty buffers from the recovery journal), checkpoint_rollback_item_count (0 — no rollback needed), and evidence_only_item_count (0). session_execution_posture_by_pane cites transcript_restored_not_rerun on two terminal panes (no silent re-run), reconnect_available on one debug pane, and rerun_required on a task pane. The three primary actions restore_card.restore_now, restore_card.skip_once, and restore_card.open_clean render distinctly. This fixture demonstrates §7.2 rule 1 (no flattening), rule 2 (exact_restore forbids silent re-run even when the advertised level is compatible_restore), and rule 3 (evidence-only count would render separately if non-zero).
